findings.evidence: `Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Subgroup/Map.lean:433` has `def mapSubgroup (f : G ≃* H) : Subgroup G ≃o Subgroup H`; this PR instead proves only selected consequences at lines 103, 110, 124, 134, and 144.
findings.fix: Add `basepointChangeSubgroupOrderIso (γ) : Subgroup (FundamentalGroup X x₀) ≃o Subgroup (FundamentalGroup X x₁) := (_root_.FundamentalGroup.fundamentalGroupMulEquivOfPath γ).mapSubgroup`, define transport as its application, and derive the current lemmas from it, adding the corresponding `symm`/`sup`/`iSup`/`iInf` API as needed.
findings.issue: `basepointChangeSubgroup` is only a function `H.map ...`, followed by special-case `bot`/`top`/`inf`/`le`/`injective` lemmas; the natural Mathlib-level object is the full order isomorphism on subgroup lattices.

summary: The subgroup transport is at the right mathematical object, but the API is under-generalized: it restates selected order facts instead of exposing the natural order isomorphism induced by the basepoint-change `MulEquiv`.
